objective: Scan the BirdCLEF 2026 public code leaderboard and adjacent BirdCLEF winning-solution material to extract the strongest currently visible modeling, preprocessing, external-data, pseudo-labeling, and ensembling patterns relevant to maximizing leaderboard score.
success_criteria: Return a ranked shortlist of public notebook patterns that appear most competitive for BirdCLEF 2026 as of 2026-04-06.
Separate observations into: backbone choice, preprocessing, augmentation, fold strategy, thresholding, external data, pseudo-labeling, and ensemble design.
Flag any claims that are inferred from notebook structure rather than explicitly documented.
Provide concrete citations or notebook links for every nontrivial claim.
evidence_requirements: Every technique claim must include a source URL and access date.
Clearly mark which findings are directly stated versus inferred from public code.
If the public code page is inaccessible or incomplete, say so explicitly and summarize what was still recoverable.
stop_conditions: BirdCLEF 2026 public notebook pages cannot be accessed with sufficient detail.
A cited notebook appears to violate competition rules or uses unavailable private assets.
The scan cannot distinguish BirdCLEF 2026 material from prior-year material.
